infuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • infuse: A Quick Start Guide

    Extend the tail of a distribution using Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD)

    c_extendtail.Rd

    This function takes existing observation vectors and extends them by generating extra values based on GPD parameters. It combines the original data with newly generated tail data.

    c_extendtail(uni, w, mlesi, mlesh, extra_n)

    Arguments

    uni

    A NumericVector of existing observation values.

    w

    A NumericVector of existing probabilities/weights.

    mlesi

    Double; the scale parameter for the GPD.

    mlesh

    Double; the shape parameter for the GPD.

    extra_n

    Double; the number of extra points to generate for the tail.

    Value

    A named list containing:

    • uni: A combined numeric vector of original and extended values.

    • w: A combined numeric vector of associated probabilities/weights.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
data(ipilimumab)
dat <- ipilimumab

### Read the data
X <- dat %>% filter(arm=="ipilimumab") %>% pull(time)
Xs <- dat %>% filter(arm=="ipilimumab") %>% pull(event)

### Remove the last few events to illustrate the functionality
wher <- X>26
Xs[wher] <- 0

### Estimate and plot the Kaplan-Meier estimation of the ECDF
fit <- c_ecdf_surv(X,Xs)
plot(w~uni,data=fit,type="s",ylim=c(0,1))
abline(h=1,lty=3)

### Estimate the scale and shape parameters for the GPD
p <- param_gpd(X,Xs)

### Extend the tail
uni <- fit$uni
w <- fit$w
mlesi <- p$mlesi
mlesh <- p$mlesh
ta <- c_extendtail(uni,w,mlesi,mlesh,p$extra_n)
ta

### plot the extension
plot(w~uni,data=ta,type="s",col="red",ylim=c(0,1),xlim = c(0,max(ta$uni)))
points(w~uni,data=fit,type="s")
abline(h=1,lty=3)

###############
### Example 2
###############
### Read the data
X <- dat %>% filter(arm=="placebo") %>% pull(time)
Xs <- dat %>% filter(arm=="placebo") %>% pull(event)

### Remove the last few events to illustrate the functionality
wher <- X>10
Xs[wher] <- 0

### Estimate aplan-Meier estimation of the ECDF
fit <- c_ecdf_surv(X,Xs)

### Estimate the scale and shape parameters for the GPD
p <- param_gpd(X,Xs)

### Extend the tail
ta <- c_extendtail(fit$uni,fit$w,p$mlesi,p$mlesh,extra_n=10)
ta

### plot the extension
plot(w~uni,data=ta,type="s",col="red",ylim=c(0,1),xlim = c(0,max(ta$uni)))
points(w~uni,data=fit,type="s")
abline(h=1,lty=3)

### Extend the tail but only with 5 values
ta <- c_extendtail(fit$uni,fit$w,p$mlesi,p$mlesh,extra_n=5)
ta

### plot the extension
plot(w~uni,data=ta,type="s",col="red",ylim=c(0,1),xlim = c(0,max(ta$uni)))
points(w~uni,data=fit,type="s")
abline(h=1,lty=3)
} # }
